name: Once-a-Year Chocolate Chip Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Pecan halves, salted and roasted
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 300 degrees. Beat butter and sugar in a bowl of heavy-duty electric mixer until light and creamy. Add vanilla and beat until combined. Stir in flour and baking powder; mix until just incorporated. Fold in chocolate pieces and pecans. Form the dough into 6 or 7 lemon-sized balls, spacing them about 3 inches apart on an ungreased baking sheet. Using the palm of your hand, gently flatten each cookie to a thickness of about 1/2 inch. Bake cookies for about 45 minutes until lightly browned at the edges. Remove from oven and let cool on a wire rack. Yield: 6 or 7 cookies.
Note: It's essential to use high-quality chocolate for these cookies. You can purchase one (3-ounce) bar of semisweet chocolate and break it into bite-sized pieces.
